2604.24428.title: BandRouteNet: An Adaptive Band Routing Neural Network for EEG Artifact Removal
2604.24428.paper_summary: Electroencephalography (EEG) is highly susceptible to artifact contamination, such as electrooculographic (EOG) and electromyographic (EMG) interference, which severely degrades signal quality and hinders reliable interpretation in applications including neurological diagnosis, brain-computer interfaces (BCIs), etc. Effective EEG denoising remains challenging because different artifact sources exhibit diverse and temporally varying distributions, together with distinct spectral characteristics across frequency bands. To address these issues, we propose BandRouteNet, an adaptive frequency-aware neural network for EEG denoising that jointly exploits band-specific processing and full-band contextual modeling. The proposed model performs band-wise denoising to explicitly capture frequency-dependent artifact patterns. Within this framework, we introduce a routing mechanism that adaptively determines where and to what extent denoising should be applied across temporal locations within each frequency band. In parallel, a full-band conditioner directly processes the original noisy EEG to extract global temporal context, producing both conditional parameters for modulating the band-wise pathway and a coarse-grained signal-level refinement to supplement the final reconstruction. Extensive experiments on the EEGDenoiseNet benchmark dataset demonstrate that BandRouteNet outperforms other methods under EOG, EMG, and mixed-artifact conditions in terms of Relative Root Mean Square Error (RRMSE) and Signal-to-Noise Ratio Improvement (SNR$_{\text{imp}}$) under unified experimental settings, while remaining highly parameter-efficient with only 0.2M trainable parameters. These results highlight its strong potential for high-performance EEG artifact removal in resource-constrained applications.
2604.24428.paper_summary_zh: 腦電圖 (EEG) 對於工件污染高度敏感，例如眼電圖 (EOG) 和肌電圖 (EMG) 的干擾，這會嚴重降低信號質量並妨礙在神經診斷、大腦-計算機介面 (BCIs) 等應用中的可靠解釋。有效的 EEG 去噪仍然具有挑戰性，因為不同的工件來源表現出多樣且隨時間變化的分佈，並且在頻率帶之間具有不同的光譜特徵。為了解決這些問題，我們提出了 BandRouteNet，一種自適應頻率感知神經網絡，用於 EEG 去噪，該網絡共同利用帶特定處理和全帶上下文建模。所提出的模型執行帶別去噪，以明確捕捉頻率依賴的工件模式。在此框架內，我們引入了一種路由機制，該機制自適應地決定在每個頻率帶內的時間位置上應該在哪裡以及在多大程度上應該應用去噪。與此同時，全帶調節器直接處理原始噪聲 EEG，以提取全局時間上下文，生成調節帶別途徑的條件參數和粗粒度信號級別的細化，以補充最終重建。對 EEGDenoiseNet 基準數據集的廣泛實驗表明，BandRouteNet 在統一實驗設置下，無論是在 EOG、EMG 還是混合工件條件下，在相對均方根誤差 (RRMSE) 和信號噪聲比改進 (SNR$_{\text{imp}}$) 方面均優於其他方法，同時僅具有 0.2M 的可訓練參數，保持高度的參數效率。這些結果突顯了其在資源受限應用中進行高性能 EEG 工件去除的強大潛力。
2604.24428.author: Phat Lam et.al.
